licensing.board: Alabama Licensing Board for General Contractors
requirements: Pass trade exam and business law exam
Financial statement required for projects over $150,000

lien.preliminary_notice: Not required for prime contractors
lien.deadline: Within 6 months of last furnishing labor/materials

taxes.contractor_tax: Use tax may apply on materials

licensing.board: Alaska Department of Commerce, Community, and Economic Development
requirements: General Contractor Residential: projects up to $25,000

lien.preliminary_notice: Not required
lien.deadline: Within 90 days of last furnishing labor/materials

taxes.sales_tax: No state sales tax (local taxes may apply)
taxes.contractor_tax: Contractor tax applies to certain projects

licensing.board: Arizona Registrar of Contractors
requirements: Pass trade exam and business management exam

lien.preliminary_notice: Required within 20 days of first furnishing

taxes.contractor_tax: Prime contractors exempt from sales tax on materials

licensing.board: California Contractors State License Board (CSLB)
requirements: Pass trade exam and law/business exam

lien.preliminary_notice: Required within 20 days of first furnishing (subcontractors/suppliers)

taxes.contractor_tax: Materials taxable; labor generally not taxable

licensing.board: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR)
requirements: Specialty trades require licenses (electrical, plumbing, HVAC)
Insurance recommended but not always required

lien.preliminary_notice: Required for residential projects (subcontractors)
lien.deadline: Within 3 months (residential) or 4 months (commercial) of last furnishing
lien.foreclosure: Within 1 year of filing lien (residential) or 2 years (commercial)

taxes.contractor_tax: Materials taxable; labor for new construction exempt

licensing.board: Florida Department of Business and Professional Regulation
requirements: Certified license: work anywhere in Florida
Registered license: work in specific local jurisdiction
Pass trade exam and business finance exam

lien.preliminary_notice: Not required for prime contractors

taxes.contractor_tax: Materials taxable; labor generally not taxable

licensing.board: NYC Department of Buildings (varies by locality)
requirements: NYC requires Home Improvement Contractor license
Home improvement projects over $200 require license in NYC
Insurance and bonding requirements vary by locality

lien.preliminary_notice: Not required
lien.deadline: Within 8 months of last furnishing (residential: 4 months)

taxes.sales_tax: 4% state + local rates (up to 8.875% in NYC)

licensing.board: Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation (local licensing)
requirements: Local jurisdictions set their own requirements

lien.preliminary_notice: Notice of Intent to Lien required within 90 days of last furnishing (subcontractors/suppliers)

taxes.sales_tax: 6.25% state + local rates (up to 11% in Chicago)
taxes.contractor_tax: Materials taxable; labor generally exempt for new construction

licensing.board: Georgia State Licensing Board for Residential and General Contractors
requirements: License required for residential and general contracting

lien.preliminary_notice: Notice to Owner/Contractor required within 30 days for some claimants
lien.foreclosure: Within 365 days of filing lien (must file commencement of action within 30 days of suit)

taxes.contractor_tax: Materials taxable; labor generally not taxable

licensing.board: North Carolina Licensing Board for General Contractors
requirements: License required for projects $40,000 or more

lien.preliminary_notice: Notice to Lien Agent required within 15 days of first furnishing (subcontractors/suppliers)

taxes.contractor_tax: Materials taxable; labor exempt for real property contracts

licensing.board: Washington State Department of Labor & Industries
requirements: Registration required for any work over $500
General Contractor or Specialty Contractor registration
Surety bond required ($12,000 for general contractors)

lien.preliminary_notice: Model Disclosure Statement required for residential; Notice to Owner within 60 days for commercial (subcontractors)

taxes.contractor_tax: Retail sales tax applies to construction services

licensing.board: No state-level licensing (local jurisdictions)
requirements: Denver, Boulder, and other cities require local licenses
General liability insurance typically required locally

lien.preliminary_notice: Notice of Intent to Lien required at least 10 days before filing lien
lien.deadline: Within 4 months (residential) or 6 months (commercial) of last furnishing
lien.foreclosure: Within 6 months of project completion or last furnishing

taxes.contractor_tax: Materials taxable; labor exempt for new construction

licensing.board: Ohio Construction Industry Licensing Board (OCILB) / Local jurisdictions
requirements: Specialty trades (HVAC, electrical, plumbing, hydronics) state-licensed
Must pass Ohio Home Improvement Contractor Exam for HIC

lien.preliminary_notice: Notice of Furnishing required within 21 days of first furnishing (subcontractors/suppliers)

licensing.board: No state-level licensing (local jurisdictions)
requirements: Home improvement contractors must register with Attorney General
Required for contractors doing $5,000+ in home improvements annually
Local municipalities set their own requirements
Insurance and OSHA training often required locally

lien.preliminary_notice: Notice of Intent may be sent before filing

taxes.contractor_tax: Materials taxable; labor generally exempt for new construction

licensing.board: Michigan Department of Licensing and Regulatory Affairs
requirements: Residential builders and maintenance/alteration contractors must be licensed
3 years experience in work classification required
Commercial contractors: no state license (local only)

lien.preliminary_notice: Notice of Furnishing within 20 days recommended

licensing.board: Virginia Department of Professional and Occupational Regulation (DPOR)
requirements: Class A: Projects over $120,000; Class B: $10K-$120K; Class C: under $10K
Trade exam may be required depending on classification
General liability and workers' comp insurance required

lien.preliminary_notice: Notice to Owner required at time of filing for subcontractors

licensing.board: Massachusetts Office of Consumer Affairs and Business Regulation

lien.preliminary_notice: Notice of Contract required for residential (record within 30 days of contract)
lien.deadline: Within 90 days of last furnishing (residential) or 60 days (commercial)
lien.foreclosure: Within 90 days of recording Statement of Account

licensing.board: New Jersey Division of Consumer Affairs
requirements: Home Repair Contractor license required for financed contracts
Must register with Division of Consumer Affairs
Local municipalities may have additional requirements

lien.preliminary_notice: Notice of Unpaid Balance required within 60 days for residential

licensing.board: Tennessee Board for Licensing Contractors
requirements: License required for projects $25,000 or more
Must pass Tennessee Business and Law exam
Trade exam required for many classifications
License required before bidding or contracting

lien.preliminary_notice: Notice of Nonpayment may be sent before filing

licensing.board: No state-level licensing (local jurisdictions)
requirements: Local jurisdictions set their own requirements
Insurance and bonding typically required locally

lien.preliminary_notice: Notice of Intent to File Lien may be sent
lien.deadline: Within 60 days (residential) or 90 days (commercial) of last furnishing

licensing.board: No state-level licensing (local jurisdictions)
requirements: Local jurisdictions set their own requirements
Kansas City and St. Louis require licenses

lien.preliminary_notice: Notice of Intent to Lien required 10 days before filing

licensing.board: Wisconsin Department of Safety and Professional Services (DSPS)
requirements: Dwelling Contractor Certification required for permits
Must pass exam for Qualifier License

lien.preliminary_notice: Notice of Intent to File Claim of Lien at least 30 days before filing

licensing.board: Minnesota Department of Labor and Industry

lien.preliminary_notice: Preliminary notice recommended but not required

licensing.board: South Carolina Contractor's Licensing Board
requirements: License required for commercial construction over $10,000
Must pass Business and Law exam
General liability and workers' comp insurance required

lien.preliminary_notice: Notice of Intent to Lien may be sent

licensing.board: Nevada State Contractors Board
requirements: License required for all construction work
Must pass trade exam and business/law exam

lien.preliminary_notice: Notice of Right to Lien within 30 days of first furnishing (optional but recommended)

licensing.board: Arkansas Contractors Licensing Board
requirements: License required for commercial projects over $50,000
Pass Arkansas Business and Law Exam

lien.preliminary_notice: Not required for prime contractors

licensing.board: Connecticut Department of Consumer Protection
requirements: Home Improvement Contractor license required for residential

lien.preliminary_notice: Notice of Intent required for residential (subcontractors)

licensing.board: Delaware Division of Professional Regulation
requirements: Business license required from Division of Revenue

lien.preliminary_notice: Not required

taxes.contractor_tax: Gross receipts tax applies to contractors

licensing.board: DC Department of Licensing and Consumer Protection
requirements: General Contractor license required for commercial

lien.preliminary_notice: Notice of Intent required 30 days before filing

requirements: Pass trade exam and business law exam

lien.preliminary_notice: Not required

licensing.board: Iowa Division of Labor
requirements: Local jurisdictions may require additional licensing

lien.preliminary_notice: Notice of Commencement recommended

lien.preliminary_notice: Not required

requirements: Pass trade exam and business law exam

lien.preliminary_notice: Not required
lien.deadline: Within 60 days of filing notice of contract termination

requirements: Local jurisdictions may have additional requirements

requirements: Omaha and Lincoln require contractor registration

requirements: Contractor licensing required in some municipalities
